description: Science progression map — descriptor codes and progression checkpoints by year level. Used for prior knowledge lookups and the ACARA Progressions visualiser.
subject: Science

standards.title: Features of Plants and Animals
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students observe external features of plants and animals and group them based on those features

standards.title: How Objects Move
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe how objects move and explain how size, shape and material influence movement

standards.title: Materials and Their Properties
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students recognise that objects are made of materials and describe the observable properties of those materials

standards.title: Basic Needs of Living Things
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students identify the basic needs of plants and animals and describe how their habitat meets those needs

standards.title: Daily and Seasonal Changes
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe daily and seasonal environmental changes and explain how these affect everyday life

standards.title: Pushes and Pulls
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe pushes and pulls in terms of strength and direction and predict their effects on motion and shape

standards.title: Earth in the Solar System
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students recognise Earth as a planet and identify patterns in the changing positions of the sun, moon, planets and stars

standards.title: Sound and Vibration
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain that sound is produced by different actions and that sound energy causes objects to vibrate

standards.title: Physical Changes to Materials
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students recognise that physical actions change the shape of materials without changing their composition

standards.title: Living and Non-living Things
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students compare characteristics of living and non-living things and explain differences between plant and animal life cycles

standards.title: Soils, Rocks and Minerals
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students compare observable properties of soils, rocks and minerals and explain their importance as Earth resources

standards.title: Heat Energy and Temperature
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students identify heat energy sources and explain how temperature changes when heat energy transfers between objects

standards.title: States of Matter and Heat
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe observable properties of solids and liquids and explain how adding or removing heat energy causes changes of state

standards.title: Data and Scientific Explanations
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how data is used to develop scientific explanations

standards.title: Science Solving Problems
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how scientific knowledge is applied to meet needs and solve problems

standards.title: Food Chains and Habitats
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain the roles of consumers, producers and decomposers in a habitat and represent feeding relationships using food chains

standards.title: Water Cycle
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe the key processes of the water cycle including precipitation, evaporation and condensation and trace the movement of water through sky, landscape and ocean

standards.title: Forces and Motion
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how frictional, gravitational and magnetic forces are exerted between objects and describe their effects on motion

standards.title: Properties of Materials
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how the properties of natural and made materials determine their suitability for different uses

standards.title: Data and Scientific Explanations
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how data is used to develop and support scientific explanations

standards.title: Science Solving Problems
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how scientific knowledge is applied to meet needs and solve real-world problems

standards.title: Structural Features and Survival
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how specific structural features and behaviours of living things are adaptations that enable survival in particular habitats

standards.title: Earth Surface Changes
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe how weathering, erosion, transportation and deposition change Earth's surface over slow and rapid timescales

standards.title: Light and Shadows
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain that light travels in straight paths from sources and describe how shadows, reflection and refraction result from this

standards.title: Particle Model of Matter
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use a particle model to explain the observable properties of solids, liquids and gases in terms of particle motion and arrangement

standards.title: Collaboration in Science
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain why scientific advances often depend on collaboration and building on prior work

standards.title: Science in Decision Making
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how scientific knowledge informs individual and community problem identification, responses and decision making

standards.title: Habitats and Living Things
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students analyse how physical conditions of a habitat affect the growth and survival of living things, and explain the relationship between changing conditions and organism responses

standards.title: Earth, Planets and Cyclic Phenomena
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students model and explain how Earth's tilt, rotation and revolution produce cyclic observable phenomena including variable day and night length

standards.title: Electrical Circuits and Energy
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how energy is transferred and transformed in electrical circuits and describe the role of components, insulators and conductors

standards.title: Reversible and Irreversible Changes
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students compare reversible and irreversible changes, explaining what distinguishes them and identifying evidence that new substances have been produced

standards.title: Classification and Biodiversity
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain why classification systems are used, apply and construct dichotomous keys, and evaluate how classification tools order and organise biodiversity.

standards.title: Food Webs and Ecosystems
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use food web models to represent matter and energy flow, and predict and explain population impacts of changes to abiotic and biotic factors.

standards.title: Earth-Sun-Moon System
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students model and explain how cyclic changes in Earth-sun-moon positions cause eclipses, seasons and tides.

standards.title: Forces and Motion
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students represent and explain how balanced and unbalanced forces, including gravity, relate to changes in an object's motion in terms of mass, magnitude and direction.

standards.title: Particle Theory and Properties
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use particle theory to explain and predict the physical properties of substances in terms of particle arrangement, motion and attraction.

standards.title: Pure Substances and Mixtures
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use a particle model to explain the difference between pure substances and mixtures, and select and apply appropriate separation techniques based on properties of substances.

standards.title: Cells as Basic Units
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students recognise cells as the basic units of living things, compare plant and animal cells, and describe the functions of specialised structures and organelles

standards.title: Structure, Function and Organ Systems
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students analyse structure-function relationships across cells, tissues and organs and explain how organ systems enable individual survival

standards.title: Plate Tectonics
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain geological features formed at plate boundaries and describe the evidence supporting plate tectonic theory

standards.title: Rock Cycle
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe rock cycle processes and explain how rock properties reflect formation history and determine use

standards.title: Energy Transfer and Transformation
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students classify energy as kinetic or potential and explain energy transfers and transformations in simple systems

standards.title: Elements, Compounds and Mixtures
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students classify matter as elements, compounds or mixtures and use symbols, formulas and models to represent them

standards.title: Physical and Chemical Changes
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students compare physical and chemical changes and identify indicators of energy change in chemical reactions

standards.title: Body Systems and Negative Feedback
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how body systems coordinate responses to stimuli and describe the operation of negative feedback mechanisms

standards.title: Reproduction and Species Survival
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe reproductive structures and processes in animals and plants and analyse how sexual and asexual reproduction enable species survival

standards.title: Carbon Cycle and Earth's Spheres
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students represent the carbon cycle and explain how combustion, photosynthesis and respiration depend on interactions between Earth's spheres

standards.title: Wave and Particle Models
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use wave and particle models to describe energy transfer and evaluate the usefulness of each model for explaining different phenomena

standards.title: Conservation of Energy and Efficiency
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students apply conservation of energy to analyse system efficiency in terms of energy inputs, outputs, transfers and transformations

standards.title: Atomic Model and Radioactive Decay
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how atomic theory developed through discovery of subatomic particles and describe how radioactive decay produces stable atoms

standards.title: Chemical Equations and Conservation of Mass
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students model atom rearrangement in chemical reactions using word and balanced equations and demonstrate conservation of mass

standards.title: Genetics and Inheritance
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain the roles of meiosis, mitosis, chromosomes, DNA and genes in heredity and predict Mendelian inheritance patterns

standards.title: Evolution by Natural Selection
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use natural selection theory to explain biological diversity and analyse the evidence supporting evolution

standards.title: Big Bang Theory
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students describe how the big bang theory models the universe's origin and evolution and analyse the supporting evidence

standards.title: Global Climate Change
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students use energy flow models between Earth's spheres to explain patterns of global climate change

standards.title: Newton's Laws of Motion
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students apply Newton's laws of motion and quantitatively analyse the relationship between force, mass and acceleration

standards.title: Periodic Table and Atomic Structure
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students explain how atomic structure and properties determine the organisation of elements in the periodic table

standards.title: Reaction Types and Rates
standards.progression_checkpoint: Students identify patterns in synthesis, decomposition and displacement reactions and explain factors that affect reaction rates

progression_threads.description: Conceptual progression trees from Foundation to Year 10, organised by science strand.
